claim: Wu Wei (無爲) is a central concept in Taoist philosophy meaning 'non-action' or 'effortless action'
Zhuangzi's 'Butterfly Dream' parable questions the boundary between dreaming and waking reality
The Stoic dichotomy of control teaches that some things are up to us (our judgments, choices) and others are not
Buddhist non-attachment (upādāna) is considered a source of suffering in the Four Noble Truths
Bill Walsh wrote 'The Score Takes Care of Itself' based on his San Francisco 49ers coaching philosophy
The default mode network (DMN) is a brain network active during mind-wandering and self-referential thought
Flow state (Csikszentmihalyi) is characterized by complete absorption in an activity, loss of self-consciousness, and intrinsic reward
The Yerkes-Dodson law describes an inverted-U relationship between arousal and performance
The Serenity Prayer by Reinhold Niebuhr asks for serenity to accept what cannot be changed, courage to change what can, and wisdom to know the difference
Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) uses acceptance and mindfulness to increase psychological flexibility
The paradoxical theory of change (Gestalt) states that authentic change occurs when one fully becomes what they are, not when they try to become what they are not
Toxic positivity is the dismissal of negative emotions as inherently harmful or inappropriate
Transient hypofrontality is a proposed neurological mechanism of flow states, where prefrontal cortex activity is temporarily downregulated
Self-determination theory (Deci & Ryan) identifies autonomy, competence, and relatedness as three basic psychological needs
The just-world hypothesis (Lerner) is the tendency to believe that people get what they deserve
Phil Jackson integrated Zen Buddhist principles into his NBA coaching, winning 11 championships
'Que Sera Sera' (Whatever will be, will be) was a popular song by Doris Day from Alfred Hitchcock's 'The Man Who Knew Too Much'
Spiritual bypass is the use of spiritual beliefs to avoid unresolved psychological issues
Reddit r/Stoicism users commonly report that the dichotomy of control helps reduce anxiety about external events
Reddit users in r/GetMotivated and r/Basketball share stories of how Bill Walsh's 'Score Takes Care of Itself' philosophy helped them focus on process over outcomes
The phrase 'take care of the pennies and the pounds will take care of themselves' is attributed to multiple sources, including a proverb about thrift
Self-regulation and ego depletion theory (Baumeister) suggests that self-control is a limited resource
The 12-step program of Alcoholics Anonymous involves surrender to a higher power as a key step toward recovery
Quiet quitting describes employees doing the minimum required without emotional investment in their jobs
